channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Geopolitical news headlines curated by AI

item.title: 🇷🇺🇸🇪 Russia suspected of using Swedish church near airport for espionage activities
item.description: Swedish intelligence services suspect Moscow is using a Russian Orthodox church near Vasteras airport as a platform for espionage and hybrid activities, warning it could threaten national security.

item.title: ✈️ Only 25% of F-35 fleet fully mission capable as readiness rates decline
item.description: A GAO report reveals the F-35's full mission capable rate fell to 25% through fiscal 2025, with readiness rates continuing to decline amid ongoing maintenance and sustainment challenges.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇪🇺 US plans major reduction of jets and warships for NATO operations in Europe
item.description: The United States plans to significantly reduce the aircraft and warships it makes available for NATO operations in Europe, according to a New York Times report.

item.title: ⚔️❄️ Russia building major troop deployment infrastructure along NATO's Northern Flank
item.description: Russia is constructing facilities for tens of thousands of troops near the Arctic, while NATO bolsters defenses with a new Arctic combat force as tensions rise on the alliance's northern border.

item.title: ✈️🇩🇪🇪🇸 Germany and Spain launch 'Team Gen 6' after FCAS fighter program collapses
item.description: Germany and Spain are forming a new coalition to develop sixth-generation fighter technology after the Franco-German FCAS program collapsed over irreconcilable differences on requirements.

item.title: ✈️ Twelve Killed in Missouri Skydiving Plane Crash
item.description: A plane carrying a pilot and 11 passengers on a skydiving outing crashed near Butler Memorial Airport in Missouri, killing all aboard. The aircraft caught fire shortly after takeoff around 11:30 AM local time.

item.title: 🌍 G7 Summit Begins in France as Anti-G7 Protests Erupt in Geneva
item.description: World leaders convened for the G7 summit in France as thousands of protesters clashed with police in Geneva. Swiss authorities deployed water cannons and tear gas to disperse demonstrators ahead of the meeting.

item.title: ⛪ Kyiv's historic Pechersk Lavra monastery burns as Russian missiles strike capital
item.description: One of Ukraine's most sacred Orthodox sites caught fire during a Russian ballistic missile attack on Kyiv, with church leaders calling it "a crime against humanity, against history, against Christianity."

item.title: 🇦🇲 Armenia releases final election results as pro-Russian opposition demands new vote
item.description: Prime Minister Pashinyan's Civil Contract party won 64 of 105 parliamentary seats, but pro-Russian opposition groups are contesting the results and demanding new elections amid tensions over Armenia's pivot away from Moscow.

item.title: ⚓ Russian Black Sea Fleet command plans relocation from Crimea to Novorossiysk
item.description: Pro-Ukrainian partisans claim Russian naval command is preparing to relocate its headquarters from occupied Crimea to the Russian mainland port of Novorossiysk, citing increasing Ukrainian strikes on the peninsula.

item.title: 🇮🇹 Italian former general launches far-right party challenging Meloni government
item.description: Roberto "Il Generale" Vannacci has founded a fast-rising far-right party that poses a threat to Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni's centre-right coalition and could reshape Italy's political landscape ahead of elections.

item.title: 🇨🇭 Swiss voters reject proposal to cap population at 10 million
item.description: Nearly 55% of Swiss voters rejected a right-wing initiative to limit the population by cutting migration, in a referendum that framed the issue around affordability and sustainability.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇷🇺🇺🇦 Trump tells Putin he is prepared to help end war in Ukraine
item.description: In a friendly and frank phone call, Trump also said the US is nearing a peace deal with Iran, according to a Putin adviser.

item.title: 🇧🇷💔 US musician Oliver Tree among six killed in Brazil helicopter collision
item.description: Two helicopters collided midair over Rio de Janeiro, killing all six people on board including the alternative singer and internet personality Oliver Tree, 32.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇺🇸 China arrests US academic at conference for espionage activities
item.description: Beijing authorities detained an American academic during a conference in China, accusing them of espionage, amid escalating US-China tensions.

item.title: 🇬🇧🇷🇺 British Royal Marines Seize Russian Shadow Fleet Oil Tanker
item.description: UK forces intercepted an oil tanker in the English Channel suspected of being part of Russia sanctioned shadow fleet. Six-hour operation marks first time Britain acted alone to stop such a vessel.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇷 US and Iran Reach Ceasefire Agreement to End War
item.description: The deal halts fighting for 60 days, opens the Strait of Hormuz, and lifts the US naval blockade on Iranian ports. Pakistan mediated the agreement, with official signing scheduled in Geneva.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Israel Expands Military Control by 1,000sq km Across Three Fronts
item.description: Al Jazeera investigation reveals Israel has expanded military control by 1,000 square kilometers in Gaza, Lebanon, and Syria, using land grabs to mask strategic failures.

item.title: 🇬🇧🇯🇵 UK and Japan Sign £18bn Investment Deal
item.description: Japanese firms will invest billions in UK infrastructure and offshore wind projects. The agreement strengthens bilateral economic ties amid shifting global trade dynamics.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukrainian Drone Strike Kills One at Russian Port of Temryuk
item.description: A Ukrainian drone attack killed one person and sparked a fire at a sea terminal in the southern Russian port of Temryuk in the Krasnodar region, according to the regional governor.

item.title: 🇻🇪 US Military Strike Kills Leader of Venezuela's Tren de Aragua Gang
item.description: President Trump announced that US forces carried out a strike killing Hector Rusthenford Guerrero Flores (Niño Guerrero), leader of the Venezuelan prison gang Tren de Aragua. The gang has expanded across Latin America and the US.

item.title: 🇱🇧 Hezbollah Expects Lebanon to Be Included in US-Iran Peace Deal
item.description: A senior Hezbollah politician expressed confidence that Iran will insist on including Lebanon in any agreement with the United States, as hopes grow for a potential peace deal between Tehran and Washington.

item.title: 🇦🇱 Kushner Albania Project Built on Disputed Land, Villagers Say
item.description: Villagers in southern Albania claim a development project tied to Jared Kushner is being built on disputed land, with locals reporting being blocked from accessing their property by armed guards.

item.title: 🇨🇳 China's New Loans Miss Forecasts as Property Slump Curbs Demand
item.description: Chinese bank lending rose less than expected in May as the country's prolonged property downturn continued to weigh on household borrowing, missing analyst forecasts.

item.title: 🇱🇧 Hezbollah confronts Israeli troops advancing in southern Lebanon amid ongoing strikes
item.description: Hezbollah fighters fired rockets at Israeli troops near Majdal Zoun, forcing them to retreat, as Israel continued air strikes and issued evacuation warnings for 20 towns in southern Lebanon including Nabatieh.
